headline: Record chip rally adds $2 trillion to Micron, Intel, AMD in Q2
detail: A sector-wide semiconductor surge of this magnitude validates the bullish trend under SMH and SOXL and gives fundamental wind behind your concentrated tech book. However, a $2 trillion Q2 run also raises the risk of a mean-reversion pullback - extended rallies after big quarters can see profit-taking. Your SMH (up 3.07%) and SOXL (up 4.8%) are direct beneficiaries but are also most exposed if the rally pauses.
rec: Hold SMH and SOXL but tighten stops - SOXL stop could move up from 200 toward 220-230 given the gain. No new add here; chasing after a $2T run-up is exactly the wrong time.

headline: Investors piled into ETFs at record pace in first half of 2026
detail: Record ETF inflows signal broad retail and institutional participation in the market, which supports the trend in QQQ, SMH, and your index holdings. Sustained inflows are a structural tailwind but also a contrarian caution flag - peak inflow periods can precede crowded-trade unwinds. Your QQQ (up 3.04%) and SMH (up 3.07%) sit right in the sweet spot of where that money is flowing.
rec: No action needed right now - your QQQ and SMH positions are already capturing this trend. Just keep your stops in place; if inflows reverse, these are the first to see fast outflows.

headline: Trump discloses expanding financial empire including crypto
detail: The disclosure of significant crypto-related income ($580M+) and other assets from Trump keeps crypto and risk-asset sentiment elevated, which indirectly supports tech and growth names. It also adds headline noise around policy and potential conflicts of interest that could create sudden market volatility. Not a direct mover for your chip names, but macro sentiment stays risk-on for now.
rec: No trade warranted from this headline alone - it reinforces the current risk-on mood but doesn't give a specific entry or exit signal for any of your holdings.

headline: Trump Council of Economic Advisers head Pierre Yared is leaving
detail: Unexpected turnover at a key economic advisory role introduces a small but real uncertainty around macro policy continuity, which can briefly rattle rate-sensitive growth and tech names. This is the kind of headline that doesn't move markets much on its own but could amplify a down move if combined with other negative catalysts. Worth monitoring if more senior economic team exits follow.
rec: No immediate trade - just stay alert; if you see QQQ or SPY start breaking intraday support alongside more policy-uncertainty headlines, that's your cue to revisit stop levels on your index holdings.
